strengths: best-in-class text rendering — third-party tests cite ~99% character accuracy across Latin/CJK/Hindi/Bengali (number is third-party, not OpenAI-published)
weaknesses: REGRESSION vs 1.5: `background:"transparent"` is NOT supported and 400s — route transparent jobs to gpt-image-1.5
active noise-artifact bug on structured surfaces (clouds, skin, panels) per OpenAI Community thread
pricing not yet on OpenAI's pricing page — cost_hint is third-party
no streaming, no function calling, no fine-tuning, no structured outputs
models.cost_hint: TBD — OpenAI's pricing page does not yet list gpt-image-2 (verified 2026-04-26). Third-party aggregators report ~$0.21/img high-quality 1024² mirroring gpt-image-1; treat as unverified placeholder.

strengths: best text rendering of any model (LM Arena #1, ELO 1264)

models.cost_hint: $0.03/img (Vertex AI). No free API tier as of 2025-12. Deprecated path — prefer imagen-4 or gemini-3-*-image-preview for new integrations.

strengths: strongest Google photoreal — industry leader per Q1 2026 roundups
Vertex AI endpoint accepts `negativePrompt` (Gemini API endpoint silently ignores it)
weaknesses: no native RGBA (renders checkerboard as RGB pixels)
deprecated — model endpoints shut down 2026-06-24, broader cutoff 2026-06-30
Google's own guidance: keep text ≤25 chars for reliable rendering
models.cost_hint: No free API tier (verified at ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/pricing, Apr 2026). Paid: Imagen 4 Fast $0.02/img (1K only), Imagen 4 Standard $0.04/img (1K+2K), Imagen 4 Ultra $0.06/img (1K+2K, single-image only). DEPRECATED — endpoint shutdown 2026-06-24. Google recommends migrating to gemini-2.5-flash-image.

strengths: ranked #1 on Artificial Analysis Image Arena at launch (Elo 1302)
multimodal edit with in-context image refs (up to 14)
weaknesses: no native RGBA (renders checkerboard as RGB)
i18n languages have higher error rates than English
models.cost_hint: No free API tier as of 2025-12; paid via gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview (Nano Banana 2): $0.045/0.5K, $0.067/1K, $0.101/2K, $0.151/4K. Legacy gemini-2.5-flash-image (original Nano Banana): $0.039/img at 1K. Both are paid-only. AI Studio web UI (https://aistudio.google.com) is free for interactive use (Google does not publish a hard daily cap; community-observed 500–1,000 images/day dynamic on server load).
models.notes: text_ceiling_chars set to 50 — reflects ~90% accuracy benchmarks; the older 'garbles past 3 words' rule applied to gemini-2.5-flash-image, not to this 3.1 preview.

strengths: paragraph-length text reliable (~94-96% accuracy per benchmark roundups)
best Google model for legible long-form text — single-line error rate <10% across 15 supported languages
multi-ref brand lock up to 4 images
weaknesses: no native RGBA (renders checkerboard as RGB)
tightened Jan 2026 IMAGE_SAFETY filter — false positives on swimwear/form-fitting apparel
small faces and fine details degrade
RLHF 'too realistic' bias hurts surreal prompts
TTFT ~3.4s, full gen 20-40s; rate-limit incidents observed Jan 2026
character drift after multiple iterative edits
models.cost_hint: No free API tier as of 2025-12; Nano Banana Pro paid (verified at ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/pricing, Apr 2026): $0.134/img at 1K and 2K, $0.24/img at 4K. Plus $0.0011 per input image (560 tokens). AI Studio web UI (https://aistudio.google.com) is free for interactive use; Gemini consumer app Basic: 20 images/day, AI Plus: 50/day, AI Pro: 100/day (Google help page, Mar 2026).
models.notes: text_ceiling_chars set to 200 — Google's marketing covers 'short tagline or long paragraph' and benchmark accuracy holds across paragraph-length text. Earlier conservative ~30/50 estimates were wrong.

weaknesses: restricted license on commercial use without Stability membership

never_use_for: commercial use without BFL Dev license

strengths: 5-10 words / one tagline reliable per BFL prompting guide and third-party tests (~92% accuracy on complex layouts)
supports JSON-structured prompts, HEX colors, @-image refs
weaknesses: small glitches in long non-Latin passages
trails Nano Banana Pro on dense copy and trails Ideogram on pure typography
BFL Flux 2 prompting guide: 'FLUX.2 does not support negative prompts'
no native RGBA — matte after generation
models.cost_hint: $0.03 first MP + $0.015/extra MP on fal (1024² = $0.03, 1920×1080 = $0.045). BFL Playground similar tier. Use flux-2-flex if you need steps+guidance control (BFL claims strongest text in the line).

strengths: BFL claims strongest text rendering in the Flux 2 line
exposes step-count + guidance-scale parameters (pro variants don't)
models.cost_hint: $0.05/MP (fal flux-2-flex) — pricier than pro for the parameter knobs

strengths: 32B open weights on HF, NVIDIA + ComfyUI optimized
never_use_for: commercial use without BFL Dev license

strengths: iterative asset family from one master
weaknesses: stylized fonts can drift across edits

strengths: strongest pure-typography model in the registry
weaknesses: photoreal humans weak — multi-person scenes get deformed faces/fingers
Magic Prompt expands literal prompts (set magic_prompt_option to OFF)
Chinese / Arabic / non-Latin still being refined per Ideogram
ceiling is realistically ~3-6 words reliable, ~10 with seed retries — earlier '~80 chars' claim was over-optimistic

strengths: RGBA via dedicated POST /v1/ideogram-v3/generate-transparent endpoint with rendering_speed:TURBO (no style:transparent param exists)
native SVG output with logical groups
style_id brand lock (UUID from 5 ref images)
weaknesses: superseded by V4 for non-brand work
models.cost_hint: $0.04/img (raster), $0.08/img (vector). Keep for brand-style pipelines requiring style_id.

strengths: current SOTA native SVG (Feb 2026 release) — only paid model besides freepik-text-to-icon that emits real vector
4 SKUs: V4 (1024² raster), V4 Vector, V4 Pro (2048² raster), V4 Pro Vector
weaknesses: NO style_id or predefined style support — use V3 for brand pipelines requiring style presets
dropped style creation, prompt-based editing, image sets, artistic-level control vs V3
slower than V3: ~10s standard, ~15s Vector, ~30s Pro, ~45s Pro Vector
V4 raster transparency not explicitly enumerated in docs — verify before relying (SVG path is the safe transparent route)

models.cost_hint: paste-only in core — enterprise Firefly API is billed per-credit

strengths: fal aggregator path to Flux Pro when BFL keys unavailable
fal aggregator path to Flux 2 Pro
weaknesses: fal schema lists negative_prompt but it's a no-op (BFL Flux 2 has no negative-conditioning path)

strengths: ZERO signup, ZERO key — HTTP GET only
weaknesses: as of 2026-04-21 anonymous requests silently swap models (observed fallback to 'sana' when model=flux requested); JPEG response
models.notes: Anonymous pipeline may return a different backend than requested (observed 'sana' swap 2026-04-21). Pass POLLINATIONS_TOKEN for deterministic model selection.

weaknesses: lower identity preservation than BFL Kontext
queue-based — anonymous waits can be minutes

strengths: Flux.1 Schnell fp8 on the horde — free
HF Inference free tier — no credit card
SD3 Medium via HF Inference free tier
weaknesses: research license on SD3 Medium — check before commercial use

strengths: Flux Dev via HF Inference free tier
never_use_for: commercial use without BFL Dev license

strengths: Cloudflare Workers AI free tier (10k neurons/day)

weaknesses: quality ceiling lower than Flux.2 dev

strengths: Flux.2 dev on CF free tier — accepts reference images for brand consistency
weaknesses: highest neuron cost among CF image models

strengths: SDXL base 1.0 on CF free tier
ByteDance SDXL-Lightning — 4-step inference, ~2 neurons per image
Leonardo Phoenix 1.0 on CF free tier
Leonardo Lucid Origin — Leonardo's most adaptable preset
Replicate-hosted Flux 1.1 Pro; good fallback when BFL keys unavailable
Flux Dev via Replicate — LoRA-trainable upstream
never_use_for: commercial use without BFL Dev license

strengths: Recraft V3 native SVG output via Replicate — fallback when no direct Recraft key
Ideogram 3 via Replicate — alt path when direct Ideogram key is unavailable

weaknesses: text rendering weak past one glyph

strengths: self-host Flux.1-dev / Flux.1-schnell with brand LoRA
brand-locked output via trained LoRA (use with asset_train_brand_lora)
consistent mascot / character across an asset set
weaknesses: requires a trained LoRA to be meaningful
never_use_for: one-off assets with no brand history

strengths: Freepik flagship; realism preset by default
best prompt adherence of the Mystic family
Flux 2 Pro hosted via Freepik (no BFL key required)
covered by Freepik 5 EUR free trial
weaknesses: Freepik passes negative_prompt to BFL but BFL Flux 2 has no negative-conditioning path — silently ignored

strengths: native SVG output (joins recraft-v4 + inline_svg as the third real vector route)
5 styles: solid, outline, color, flat, sticker
produces RGBA — alternative to BiRefNet/BRIA matte for cases where the local matte fails
rebuild scene lighting via prompt, reference image, or lightmap (Magnific.ai tech)
apply artistic style with intensity control (Magnific.ai tech)
outpaint up to 2048px per edge using Flux Pro
outpaint with Seedream 4.5 — strong reference consistency
cheapest paid Flux Schnell on the market: $0.0012/img
weaknesses: FREE TIER: outputs owned by Appy Pie LLP with mandatory attribution — unsuitable for production brand assets
Cloudflare/Pollinations cover the same model with no IP clawback — prefer those for free use
never_use_for: production logos / app icons / brand marks on the FREE tier (IP clawback)

weaknesses: FREE TIER: outputs owned by Appy Pie LLP — unsuitable for production brand assets
Cloudflare Workers AI hosts SDXL Base/Lightning with no IP clawback
never_use_for: production assets on the FREE tier

strengths: inpainting use case — useful for asset_validate repair primitive when the subject mask is known
weaknesses: FREE TIER: outputs owned by Appy Pie LLP — unsuitable for production
URL pattern best-guess from operation slug — may need fixing on first call (see provider source)
